text:
Funny that you brought up Dune. I never personally got into it, but I am familiar with the setting. and yeh the Butlerian Jihad is ironically salient to events of today and my own work. I am from New Zealand where Samuel Butler published his article Darwin Among the Machines about the potential for machines to subjugate humanity as we have traditionally done to each other, animlas, and more recently machines. He also started writing his classic novel, Erewhon here too.

I worked for the StrongAI Lab or SAIL at the University of Auckland here in Aotearoa/New Zealand, ad my approach to AI is more informed by both Warren Brodey and Bret Victor.

I am from New Zealand where Samuel Butler published his article Darwin Among the Machines about the potential for machines to subjugate humanity as we have traditionally done to each other, animlas, and more recently machines. He also started writing his classic novel, Erewhon here too.

I worked for the StrongAI Lab or SAIL at the University of Auckland here in Aotearoa/New Zealand, ad my approach to AI is more informed by both Warren Brodey and Bret Victor.

text:
Currently I'm working on building a general purpose projectional editor for general purpose software development and AI applications, which I am building from open source components, especially the Graphte graphics editor with its Graphene graph language and the Pax UI framework. I was already exploring the possibility of building a projectional editor inspired by Bred Victor's thoughtpieces Learnable Programming, Media for Thinking the Unthinkable, and Humane Representations of Thought which I read years ago and found incredibly resonant and inspiring. I realized the developers of Graphite had already built one though its currently focused on graphics, I think it can easily be adapted for general purpose software development.
<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZUbcwUC5lxA>
